trade.summary: Substantial bilateral trade. Canada exports crude oil, natural gas, aircraft, precious metals, and agricultural products to the Regno Unito. The Regno Unito exports machinery, pharmaceuticals, vehicles, and financial services to Canada. Post-Brexit, both are negotiating a Canada-Regno Unito FTA.

diplomatic.summary: Canada and the Regno Unito share the deepest possible ties — Commonwealth, Five Eyes intelligence alliance, shared monarch (until 2023), parliamentary democracy tradition, and common law system. Both are NATO allies and G7 members. The relationship is one of the strongest in Canadian foreign policy.

military.summary: Both are Five Eyes partners, NATO allies, and CANZRegno Unito security partners. Extensive military cooperation including joint operations, equipment interoperability, intelligence sharing, and combined training exercises. Regno Unito and Canada cooperate closely in Arctic security.

financial.summary: London is a major financial center for Canadian companies and banks. RBC, TD, and other Canadian banks have major Regno Unito operations. British companies invest extensively in Canadian energy, mining, and finance. Bilateral investment is among the largest in the world.

energy.summary: Regno Unito-Canada energy cooperation deepened post-Brexit and post-Ucraina war. Canada is increasing LNG exports to Regno Unito. British energy companies (BP, Shell) have major Canadian upstream investments. Growing green hydrogen cooperation.

scientific.summary: Outstanding scientific partnership. Regno Unito-Canada universities exchange large numbers of researchers. Joint funding through Regno UnitoRI-NSERC programs. Collaboration in AI (Toronto-London AI corridor), quantum computing, materials science, and health research. Joint space programs.
